channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Breaking: US conducts additional strikes against IS militants in Nigeria
item.description: The US Africa Command announced the strikes days after Trump claimed to have killed the group's second-in-command.

media:group.media:description: Drone footage shared to social media by AFRICOM shows coordinated strikes against Islamic State militants in Nigeria.

item.title: Blanchett says #MeToo reckoning in Hollywood 'got killed very quickly'
item.description: The Oscar-winning actor says Hollywood's reckoning over sexual misconduct and gender equality has stalled despite the global impact of #MeToo.

media:group.media:description: Cate Blanchett at the 79th international film festival in Cannes, France.

item.title: Queensland man accused of paying to watch live child sexual abuse
item.description: Police allege the 38-year-old from the state's far north took part in more than 40 live shows on an online platform. WARNING: This story contains details of child sexual abuse.

media:group.media:description: Anti-child exploitation officers searched a home in Innisfail in January 2025.

item.title: Australia suspends new private college bids to enrol international students
item.description: Australia suspends private colleges from applying to offer new courses to international students as part of a crackdown on abuse in the student visa system.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Labor is implementing a 12-month pause on new private colleges and training organisations applying to offer courses to international students.

item.title: Overseas company makes billions in profit but is receiving millions in government bailouts
item.description: China is "playing a hard game" with its stranglehold on the global minerals and metals market and Australian smelters need taxpayer support to "play hard back".
item.dc:creator: Angelique Donnellan and Adam Langenberg

media:group.media:description: Global company Nyrstar says its metal smelter in Port Pirie, South Australia has been losing tens of millions of dollars each month.

item.title: 'Predator' arsonist's $1.5m fire an 'act of retribution', court told
item.description: The guilty man embarked on a two-week crime spree after his relationship soured with his business partner, who he also assaulted in front of the man's children.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: The fire was visible from nearby buildings.

item.title: SA premier plays down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address
item.description: SA Premier Peter Malinauskas has played down social media posts attempting to reveal his home address in the aftermath of hundreds of trees being removed for the $45 million North Adelaide Golf Course.

media:group.media:description: Premier Peter Malinauskas says most of the golf course protesters have conducted themselves civilly.

item.title: Port Kembla identified as preferred AUKUS base in secret report
item.description: A labour union has called for transparency after Port Kembla in NSW was identified as a preferred east coast location for a nuclear submarine base in newly released documents.
item.dc:creator: Grace Crivellaro

media:group.media:description: Under the AUKUS arrangement, Australia is set to buy several nuclear-powered Virginia-class submarines from the US in the 2030s.

item.title: Parent company orders cattle station to 'cease any plans' for bird cull
item.description: A cull of 20,000 native birds on a Northern Territory cattle station will not be going ahead.
item.dc:creator: Tom Robinson

media:group.media:description: A planned cull of 20,000 native birds at Newcastle Waters cattle station in the NT will no longer be going ahead.

item.title: Anglo American sells central Qld coal mines to UK company
item.description: Anglo American says it has sold its five steelmaking coal mines in central Queensland to a UK mining company Dhilmar Ltd for up to $5.43 billion.
item.dc:creator: Bryn Wakefield

media:group.media:description: The sale includes five steelmaking coal mines in central Queensland.

item.title: Alice Springs' gruelling West Macs Monster ultra-marathon, in pictures
item.description: Over distances of up to 231km, 573 runners took to the Larapinta Trail outside Alice Springs at the weekend to compete in the 2026 West Macs Monster.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Martin, Chris Murrkarany Fitzpatrick, Elsie Lange, and Will Regan

media:group.media:description: Hundreds of runners from across the country took part in the West Macs Monster run outside Alice Springs at the weekend.

item.title: Trial begins for concert pianist suing Melbourne Symphony Orchestra
item.description: Pianist Jayson Gillham is suing the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra in the Federal Court over political discrimination after comments he made about Gaza on stage during a 2024 performance.

media:group.media:description: Jayson Gillham is suing the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra for discrimination.

item.title: Treasurer says 'death tax' claims are a scare campaign, 'pure and simple'
item.description: The Coalition seizes on the federal government's broken tax promises, claiming a "death tax" has been sneaked into changes to trusts passed on through inheritance. The treasurer says that is a scare campaign.
item.dc:creator: Jake Evans

media:group.media:description: Jim Chalmers says claims the minimum tax on discretionary testamentary trusts amounted to a "death tax" were a scare campaign.

item.title: Man who moved fridge with Isla Bell's body inside once jailed for assault
item.description: The ABC can reveal Eyal Yaffe previously served time for assaulting and choking a woman who he threatened to "stuff in a suitcase".
item.dc:creator: Kristian Silva

media:group.media:description: Eyal Yaffe leaving the Supreme Court after charges relating to Isla Bell's case were dropped last week.

item.title: Man who stomped on face of off-duty police officer won't have to return to jail
item.description: Koby Weaver, 21, was sentenced last month to two years' jail, after he ran into a brawl between two of his friends and two off-duty officers.
item.dc:creator: Isobel Roe

media:group.media:description: Koby Weaver was jailed over his role in an attack which left an off-duty police officer with serious injuries.

item.title: Five of our best recipes using the cheap and humble tinned bean
item.description: From vegetarian chilli to hearty soup, these easy recipes might convince you to add beans to your regular dinner rotation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Chisholm

media:group.media:description: This vegetarian chilli can be topped with sour cream, avocado, coriander and jalapeno or whatever you desire.

item.title: For the second straight game, Slater has overlooked Walsh. But why?
item.description: Reece Walsh's generational run to end 2025 was almost without peer, but he has rejoined the ranks of the mortals to start this year.
item.dc:creator: Jon Healy

media:group.media:description: Billy Slater overlooked Reece Walsh for Game III last year.

item.title: Coal workers owed $16 million as Bowen Basin mine nears sale
item.description: Federal Court documents show employees of the Vitrinite group of companies are collectively owed more than $16 million in entitlements. The mine near Dysart in Central Queensland has faced ongoing financial difficulties, exacerbated by lower coal prices.
item.dc:creator: Liam O'Connell

media:group.media:description: A drone image of a section of the Vulcan mine.

item.title: 'This is the end': SA health chief recalls terrifying moment she was hit by car
item.description: SA public health chief Nicola Spurrier opens up about the moment when she was knocked from her bike by a car last year, and recounted the experience — which left her with broken bones and fearing for her life — only hours after a 39-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a crash in the Adelaide Hills.
item.dc:creator: Josephine Lim

media:group.media:description: Professor Spurrier says her shoulder was badly injured in the collision.

item.title: Nearly one in five Tasmanians would vote One Nation, poll shows
item.description: A recent Tasmanian poll has shown that One Nation would win a "number of seats" in the next state election.
item.dc:creator: Josh Duggan

media:group.media:description: One Nation's win in the Farrer by-election in May marked the first time the party, led by Pauline Hanson, had won a lower house seat in federal parliament.

item.title: Man's leg amputated in public park, court hears
item.description: The trial of a man who allegedly performed an amputation on a 66-year-old, causing his death, is likely to focus on the accused's "state of mind" and intention, a court hears.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Testa

media:group.media:description: John Yalu is accused of murdering Kalman Tal in an amputation Innisfail in February 2022.

item.title: Horror MotoGP crash leaves rider with broken neck
item.description: Alex Márquez is lucky to be alive after he spectacularly wrecked his MotoGP bike during the Catalan Grand Prix in Barcelona, while Johann Zarco was also taken to hospital after an equally terrifying crash later in the race.

media:group.media:description: Alex Márquez suffered a fractured C7 vertebrae and a broken collarbone.

item.title: Federal government intervenes for third time over China-linked stakes in rare earths miner
item.description: The federal treasurer orders another six companies to divest their holdings in Northern Minerals as concerns grow in Canberra that Chinese-backed investors are trying to seize control of the rare earths mining company.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic

media:group.media:description: Northern Minerals operates the Browns Range mine in WA.

item.title: Woman jailed for drunkenly setting friend on fire appeals sentence
item.description: Corbie Walpole's legal team has argued the sentencing judge failed to properly consider psychiatric evidence about her mental health at the time of the offending.
item.dc:creator: Ashlee Aldridge

media:group.media:description: Corbie Jean Walpole (centre) has appealed her seven year jail sentence in the Court of Criminal Appeal.

item.title: PM 'confident' about Whyalla amid work to bring furnace 'back to life'
item.description: Anthony Albanese says he remains "very confident" in the future of steelmaking in Whyalla, and says the federal government is "continuing to work" with lead producer Nyrstar to secure its operations in Port Pirie and Hobart.

media:group.media:description: The site's aging blast furnace has been out of action since last month.
